name: Camino Francés - Nájera Loop
name_original: Ruta por la Rioja Alta – circular desde Nájera por Azofra y Cirueña
description: Cycle the moderate 21.0-mile Camino Francés - Nájera Loop through La Rioja's vineyards and charming villages in 2 hours and 26 minutes.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nájera are situated in the La Rioja region of Spain, characterized by diverse landscapes. The area features rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and the Najerilla River, which bisects the town. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including both paved and unpaved surfaces, with distinctive red rock formations visible along some routes. Best touring cycling routes around Nájera The most popular touring cycling route is Camino Francés - Nájera Loop, a 21.0 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses La Rioja's vineyards and charming villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Grajera Park – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Nájera, a difficult 38.3 miles (61.7 km) path. This longer ride includes significant elevation gain and passes by the La Grajera Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Rollo de Azofra – Red Rocks of Nájera loop from Nájera, a 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail leading through the distinctive Red Rocks of Nájera, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Touring cycling around Nájera is defined by rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and the Najerilla River. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Nájera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nájera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nájera?
A: Nájera offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with nearly 30 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from moderate loops to challenging long-distance rides.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nájera?
A: The routes around Nájera feature diverse terrain, including both paved and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter rolling hills, extensive vineyards, and distinctive red rock formations, particularly along the Najerilla River.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Nájera?
A: Yes, Nájera has options for easier rides. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are at least two easy touring cycling routes available. For a moderate option that traverses vineyards and villages, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995572371/camino-frances-najera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés - Nájera Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landscapes I can see while cycling in Nájera?
A: Cyclists in Nájera can enjoy picturesque vineyards, the scenic Najerilla River, and the distinctive Red Rocks of Nájera. The region also features rolling hills and, nearby, the Alto Najerilla Natural Park with its mountain landscapes and waterfalls.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Nájera is rich in history. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3233076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Sonsierra Fortress Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/581395" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Davalillo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4040632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro River from Briones Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago, featuring the historic Monastery of Santa María la Real.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Nájera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nájera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic vineyards and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any challenging long-distance touring cycling routes in the Nájera area?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Nájera offers several difficult and long-distance routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villoslada de Cameros – Red Cliffs of Navarrete loop from Nájera&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 142 km and includes significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Nájera?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Nájera are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31565963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Park – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Nájera&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14939999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Azofra – Red Rocks of Nájera loop from Nájera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nájera?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the region's extensive vineyards suggest pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the landscape is vibrant with growth or harvest colors. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there routes that pass through vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The region around Nájera is characterized by extensive vineyards. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995572371/camino-frances-najera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés - Nájera Loop&lt;/a&gt; are specifically noted for traversing La Rioja's picturesque vineyards.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Red Rocks of Nájera?
A: Yes, you can cycle past the distinctive Red Rocks of Nájera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14939999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rollo de Azofra – Red Rocks of Nájera loop from Nájera&lt;/a&gt; is a great option that leads directly through this unique geological feature.

Q: Are there any routes that include a reservoir or significant water features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31565963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Park – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Nájera&lt;/a&gt; is a longer, more difficult ride that passes by the La Grajera Reservoir, offering scenic views of the water.
tips: Wherever you look, along this road that is part of the EuroVelo 3, also known as the pilgrims' route, you can enjoy an endless number of beautiful and picturesque vineyards.

In this section of the Camino de Santiago Francés, near the municipality of Cirueña, it has magical views of the popular vineyards of La Rioja, as well as the small mountains that surround this beautiful land.

This monastery, whose construction began in the 12th century, is the first female Cistercian community to settle in the Iberian Peninsula. The complex was not completed until the 20th century itself, when the rooms where the nuns live today were built. Its architectural evolution gives it traces of Romanesque, Gothic and Neoclassical styles that make it a very unique complex.

It is located a short distance from San Millán de la Cogolla and framed in what is now known as the route of the monasteries in La Rioja.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Camino Francés - Nájera Loop?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 33.7 kilometers (20.9 miles) long. It typically takes around 2 hours and 26 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Nájera and the La Rioja region features rolling hills and extensive vineyards. You can expect a mix of asphalt and gravel surfaces, often on quiet secondary roads. While there are ascents and descents, it's generally described as undulating rather than mountainous, with some moderately challenging sections, particularly between Azofra and Cirueña.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll primarily cycle through the picturesque vineyards of La Rioja, offering tranquil views. The route also passes through agricultural tracks and charming medieval villages. In spring, wildflowers add color, and in autumn, the vineyards display stunning golden hues. Don't miss the Trail Through the Vineyards of La Rioja.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Francés - Nájera Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While there are some climbs, they are generally manageable for touring cyclists with good fitness. Compared to other sections of the Camino Francés, this part is often recommended for its more manageable terrain, making it accessible for those with some cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the heart of the renowned La Rioja wine region, offering endless vineyard views. Key highlights include the Endless vineyards and the Trail Through the Vineyards of La Rioja. You'll also pass by the historically significant Monastery of Santa María de San Salvador in Nájera.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal. In spring, the fields are vibrant with wildflowers, and in autumn, the vineyards showcase beautiful golden colors. The weather during these seasons is generally pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Camino Francés - 03 Logroño a Burgos, Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas), and the Camino de Santiago Francés, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Francés - Nájera Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route largely follows public roads and agricultural tracks, specific regulations for dogs can vary by municipality and private land. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this loop. It primarily uses public roads and established paths. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Monastery of Santa María de San Salvador, there might be separate entry fees.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Nájera?
faqs.answer: Nájera, being a significant town on the Camino Francés, offers various parking options. You can typically find public parking areas within the town. It's best to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient and permitted spots near the starting point of the loop.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through charming villages and the La Rioja wine region, you'll find opportunities for refreshments. Nájera itself has cafes and restaurants, and smaller villages along the way may offer local establishments where you can experience regional gastronomy and wines.
